content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 2,2005
Dianne McHugh
Contra Costa County-Elections Department
524Main Street
Martinez, CA94553
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. ,4.-05-078
Dear Ms. McHugh:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the County of Contra Costa
for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').1
QUESTION
The County of Contra Costa is holding a mail ballot election on August 30, 2005.
May the first pre-election campaign statement in connection with the election covering the
period through July 16, 2005,be combined with the July 31,2005 serni-annual statement?
CONCLUSION
Committees involved in the August 30,2005 election may file a combined
statement that will satisff the requirement to file the semi-annual statement on July 31,
2005. This combined statement will be filed on July 21,2005, covering the period through
July 16, 2005. A filing schedule containing the filing deadlines and periods covered is
enclosed with this letter.
FACTS
The County of Contra Costa is holding a mail ballot election on August 30,2005.
The first pre-election campaign disclosure staternent in connection with the election is due
on July 21,2005, with a closing date of July 16,2005. The semi-annual statement for the
first half of 2005 is due on July 3 1 , 2005 , with a closing date of Jun e 30, 2005 .
lGovernmentCodesections81000*91014. Commissionregulations appearatTitle2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
i&iin


File No. 4-05-078
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are required
to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in
connection with that election. These pre-election campaign statements are generally filed
40 days before the election arrd again 12 daysbefore the election. (Section S+ZOO.S.;
Semi-annual statements are filed January 3l and July 31, respectively. (Secti on 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when an
overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an
additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election staternents. (Section84205.) In this
case, filing the two pre-election statements in connection with the August 30 election will
satisfu the July 31,2005, semi-annual filing requirement. A campaign statement filing
schedule which outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for your
convenience.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (866)
275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
cA .h,a ^ €
N ek/1^a.acs
By: Adrianne Korchmaros
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
